title: asclepias engelmanniana var. rusbyi
intro: Asclepias engelmanniana var. rusbyi, also known as Rusby's milkweed, is a variety of flowering plant in the milkweed family (Apocynaceae) native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can be used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Asclepias engelmanniana var. rusbyi are numerous, and it has been used to treat a range of ailments, including eczema, acne, and even certain types of cancer. Herbal preparations of Rusby's milkweed can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
